title: Is a Solar Battery Worth It?
description: A battery can provide backup power and improve self-consumption, but it adds cost.
quickAnswer: A solar battery may be worth it if outages are common, backup power matters, or your utility rewards using stored solar instead of exporting it.

scenario.title: Battery Example
details: Useful when evening rates are high

faqs.question: Can one battery power a whole home?
faqs.answer: Usually only for limited time. Whole-home backup often requires multiple batteries and load management.

faqs.question: Does a battery always improve payback?
faqs.answer: Not always. Batteries can improve resilience and may help under time-of-use rates, but they often lengthen simple payback when backup value is not included.

faqs.question: Can I add a battery later?
faqs.answer: Often yes, but compatibility depends on the inverter, electrical design, and space near the service equipment.

sources: U.S. Department of Energy homeowner solar resources
DSIRE state and utility incentive database
